posture: 113 roles · 36 strong-act · afternoon refresh (14:00) — SEARCH-ONLY FALLBACK: no Chrome browser was connected, so no JS-board listings could be browser-verified this run. All still-live morning items (browser-verified via Chrome at 08:00) carried forward unchanged; web-search discovery surfaced no new confirmable roles this afternoon (one lead, a 'Recludo Group' Head of Risk & Compliance, could not be verified as a live listing and was not added). Best actionable role remains the CFO — NSW Dept of Customer Service (Derwent-held, closing soon).
summary: Afternoon run used the search-only fallback because no Chrome browser was connected — treat any un-verified board as leads, not confirmed live roles. The 113 items carried forward were browser-verified in this morning's 08:00 run and remain the current board; nothing expired within the day and web search turned up no new confirmable Sydney/remote-AU or interstate-board roles this afternoon. A manual look at the §1a recruiter boards (Robert Half, Bluefin, Sharp & Carter, Kaizen) and Seek 'most recent' remains worthwhile, since search cannot see their JavaScript-rendered cards; the strongest live actions are the Derwent-held NSW Customer Service CFO and the recruiter-held interim finance/risk contracts.

items.headline: Independent Non-Executive Board Director — Institute of International Studies, Sydney NSW
items.summary: Independent NED appointment advertised on Seek by a Sydney-based international-studies institute; limited public detail on remit, remuneration or committee structure in the teaser.
items.so_what: A board seat fits your NED ambitions, but the organisation and terms are unclear from the listing — worth a watch and a direct enquiry to confirm scope and independence before pursuing.

items.headline: Non-Executive Director — Whiddon, Sydney (aged-care NFP)
items.summary: Independent NED sought for the board of Whiddon, a 75-year-old not-for-profit aged-care provider operating across regional, rural and remote NSW and QLD, to help guide a growth, capital-investment and transformation agenda. Whiddon is running the process internally with no external agency.
items.so_what: A genuine Sydney board seat matching your FAICD governance and risk-oversight strengths and your NFP affinity, but the board is prioritising development-led candidates with property/infrastructure/capital-development expertise you lack — a considered EOI rather than a strong fit, so watch. Browser-verified live on Seek this run (listed over the weekend).

items.headline: Independent Chairs & Members, Audit & Risk Committees — NSW Government (Sydney, prequalification panel)
items.summary: Standing NSW Government prequalification scheme through which agencies appoint independent chairs and members to their audit and risk committees.
items.so_what: Direct fit for your FCPA/FAICD audit-and-risk-committee profile and existing NED experience; register on the panel to be drawn on as agencies refresh committees.

items.summary: Board-level company secretary for a not-for-profit specialist hospital, covering governance, board papers and statutory compliance.
items.so_what: Governance/company-secretary fit close to your board background; still live from last week.

items.summary: Recruiter-managed company secretary role for a Sydney organisation, focused on board governance and statutory duties.
items.so_what: Another company-secretary option matching your governance/NED experience; recruiter-held, so register interest directly.

items.so_what: Browse for risk/audit-committee and energy/local-government boards that suit an interstate-travel cadence; situational awareness rather than a single role.

items.headline: Non-Executive Director — Australian Livestock Export Corporation (LiveCorp), Sydney (Remote)
items.summary: One independent NED sought for LiveCorp's skills-based board; the livestock export industry's research, development and marketing body. Part-time, initial three-year term, subject to member election at the AGM on 5 November 2026.
items.so_what: A properly constituted skills-based board seeking regulatory, government and commercial experience — all of which you have. The board is prioritising live-export/agricultural connection, which you lack, so treat it as a considered application rather than a shoo-in.

items.summary: Expressions of interest for President of the Cobar Water Board, a 1963 statutory authority supplying bulk water to Cobar Shire and local mining companies. Part-time, five-year term; the President is selected by the Minister for Water and members are appointed by the Governor.
items.so_what: A chair-equivalent statutory appointment blending your utilities and local-government strengths, and remote-friendly. The criteria lean on demonstrated affinity with the Cobar community, which is the main gap to address in an EOI.
